title: Yosys issue error in propagating parameter from one module to other module: ERROR: implicitly declared outside of a module.
body:
############## File: a1.sv  ##########################

include "a.svh"
module a1 (
    input   logic                                   rst_n,                      
    input   logic                                   clk                        
);
endmodule

############## File: b1.sv  ##########################

`include "a.svh"
`include "b.svh"

module b1 (
    input   logic         rst_n,      
    input   logic         clk        

);
endmodule

############  File:  a.svh ###################

`ifndef INCLUDE_A_DEFS
`define INCLUDE_A_DEFS

  parameter bit [31:0] TRIG_NUM = 4; 

`endif 

########## File: b.svh ##########################

`ifndef INCLUDE_B_DEFS
`define INCLUDE_B_DEFS

   parameter bit [31:0] MTRIG_NUM             = TRIG_NUM; 

`endif 

############  yosus Run Log ########################
 Yosys 0.9+3871 (git sha1 98afe2b7, clang 10.0.0-4ubuntu1 -fPIC -Os)


yosys> read_verilog -sv a1.sv 
1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: a1.sv
Parsing SystemVerilog input from `a1.sv' to AST representation.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\a1'.
Successfully finished Verilog frontend.

yosys> read_verilog -sv a1.sv 

2. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: b1.sv
b.svh:4: ERROR: Identifier `\TRIG_NUM' is implicitly declared outside of a module.

#########################################



## Steps to reproduce the issue
Run the yosys in following step

read_verilog -sv a1.sv
read_verilog -sv b1.sv 

b.svh:4: ERROR: Identifier `\TRIG_NUM' is implicitly declared outside of a module.

## Expected behavior
Expected behaviour was global parameter defined in previous file should have propagated to next file

## Actual behavior
Tool is issue warning:
ERROR: Identifier `\TRIG_NUM' is implicitly declared outside of a [module.]

[issue.tar.gz](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/5916954/issue.tar.gz)
